highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.111: Opus 4.7 xhigh Effort & Auto Mode for Max Subscribers
highlights.summary: Claude Code now supports a new 'xhigh' effort level for Opus 4.7, slotting between 'high' and 'max' so you can fine-tune the speed-vs-intelligence tradeoff using /effort, --effort, or model settings. Max subscribers also gain access to Auto mode with Opus 4.7, letting Claude automatically pick the best model for each task. If you upgraded and hit the 'temporarily unavailable' error, v2.1.112 patches that bug.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.110: /tui Fullscreen Mode, /focus Command & Improved Navigation
highlights.summary: The new /tui fullscreen command switches Claude Code to a flicker-free rendering mode without losing your conversation, which is a big quality-of-life win for long sessions. The old Ctrl+O behavior has been split: use Ctrl+O to toggle verbose transcript and the new /focus command to enter focus view. These UI changes make it easier to stay in flow while working on complex tasks.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
highlights.summary: Anthropic has officially announced Claude Opus 4.7, the latest and most capable model in the Opus line, now available through Claude Code. This announcement provides context for the recent v2.1.111 release features like xhigh effort and Auto mode. Developers using Claude Code should read the announcement to understand the new model's capabilities and how to get the most out of it.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.108: 1-Hour Prompt Cache TTL & Recap Feature
highlights.summary: You can now opt into a 1-hour prompt cache TTL by setting the ENABLE_PROMPT_CACHING_1H environment variable, which can significantly reduce API costs on long sessions by reusing cached context. A new recap feature also helps Claude maintain context across long conversations, reducing errors from lost context. These are especially useful for developers running Claude Code against large codebases via API key, Bedrock, or Vertex.

highlights.title: Claude Code OAuth Down for >12 Hours (HN Discussion)
highlights.summary: A Hacker News thread flagged a serious OAuth authentication bug in Claude Code v2.1.107 where users were stuck on the 'Paste code here if prompted' screen with no way to complete login. The issue was underreported outside of Reddit and Discord, highlighting the importance of checking release notes and community channels when Claude Code behaves unexpectedly. Upgrading to v2.1.112 is the recommended fix if you hit this problem.

highlights.title: Claude Code Zero-to-Hero: MCP, SubAgents, Hooks, Image Handling & Background Tasks (Bilibili)
highlights.summary: This comprehensive Chinese-language Bilibili tutorial walks through Claude Code from installation and authorization all the way to advanced topics like MCP servers, sub-agents, agent skills, hooks, image input, context management, and background task handling. Timestamped chapters make it easy to jump to the specific feature you need, covering both beginner setup and power-user workflows. An excellent structured reference for developers who want a single video covering the full Claude Code feature set.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes (YouTube)
highlights.summary: A concise 30-minute YouTube tutorial designed to get developers up to speed with Claude Code's core features quickly without sitting through a multi-hour course. This is a great starting point if you're new to Claude Code and want a time-efficient overview before diving into longer resources. Pair it with the official docs for the best beginner onboarding experience.

highlights.title: The Ultimate Claude Code Guide: MCP, Skills & More (YouTube)
highlights.summary: This YouTube guide dives deep into Claude Code's more powerful features including MCP (Model Context Protocol) server integration and agent skills, making it ideal for developers ready to go beyond basic usage. Understanding MCP lets you connect Claude Code to external tools, databases, and APIs to dramatically expand what it can automate. Watch this after getting comfortable with the basics to unlock Claude Code's full potential.

tips_of_the_day: Always update Claude Code before starting a major session — running 'npm update -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code' takes seconds and can save you from hitting known bugs like the recent OAuth issue in v2.1.107.

Use /effort xhigh when you need smarter reasoning than 'high' but want to avoid the full cost and latency of 'max' — it's the sweet spot for complex refactoring tasks where you still care about response speed.

Set ENABLE_PROMPT_CACHING_1H in your environment if you regularly work on large codebases through the API; caching context for a full hour instead of 5 minutes can dramatically cut token costs during long iterative coding sessions.
